title: polygala cruciata
intro: Polygala cruciata, also known as the bitter vetch or Polygala, is a perennial herb belonging to the Polygalaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Polygala cruciata include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, Polygala cruciata is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from respiratory issues.
